name: Kurdistan Autonomy Referendum Discussions (2019)
description: In 2019, Iranian Kurdish opposition parties engaged in discussions about pursuing an autonomy or independence referendum strategy for Iranian Kurdistan, inspired by the 2017 independence referendum held by the Kurdistan Regional Government in Iraqi Kurdistan under Masoud Barzani. The discussions involved the [Democratic Party of Iranian Kurdistan (PDKI)](/orgs/democratic-party-of-iranian-kurdistan), [PJAK](/orgs/pjak), the [Komala Party of Iranian Kurdistan](/orgs/komala-party-of-iranian-kurdistan), and other Kurdish political forces. No consensus was reached and no referendum was organised — the Iraqi Kurdistan precedent had itself been crushed by Baghdad and Tehran's joint pressure. The discussions nonetheless reflect the ongoing debate within Iranian Kurdish politics about strategic goals and the influence of regional Kurdish political developments on Iranian Kurdish opposition strategy.
editorial_notes: Never materialised. Inspired by 2017 Barzani referendum (which was itself crushed by Baghdad-Tehran pressure). Significant for Kurdish political strategy debates.
